title: JavaScript Basics
briefDescription: An introductory quiz covering the core syntax and fundamental concepts of the JavaScript programming language.

questions.title: Assignment vs Equality
questions.markdownDescription:
In JavaScript, the `=` symbol is used to **assign** a value, while the `==` or `===` symbols are used to **compare** two values. Confusing these two is a common mistake for beginners learning logic.

```javascript
let x = 5; // Assignment
```
questions.prompt: Match the operator symbol to its purpose.
questions.explanation: The single equals sign assigns, while the double/triple equals sign compares.

pairs.rationale: Used to give a variable a specific value.
Used to check if two things are exactly equal to each other.
